name: SPINAL DYSRAPHISM
chapter: DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS OF SKULL AND SPINE DISORDERS
definition: abnormal / incomplete fusion of midline embryologic mesenchymal, neurologic, bony incomplete closure of bony elements of the spine (lamina + spinous processes) posteriorly OCCULT SPINAL DYSRAPHISM cleft / tethered cord WITH skin cover SPINA BIFIDA CYSTICA posterior protrusion of all / parts of the contents of the spinal canal through a bony malformation of distal spine and cord

demographics.Location: usually in lower thoracic + lumbar spine

distribution: usually in lower thoracic + lumbar spine
discriminating_features: hypoplastic posterior elements may be present

general: hypoplastic posterior elements may be present
vertical radiolucent band just behind midportion of vertebral body; disappears mostly
widened vertebral body with butterfly configuration (AP view)
adaptation of vertebral endplates of adjacent vertebral bodies
height of fused vertebral bodies equals the sum of heights of involved bodies +
"waist" at level of intervertebral disk space
